    Tsung-Han Yang

    Postdoctoral Researcher @ ORNL
    PhD in Physics @ Brown University

    • Knoxville, TN
    • Email

    About Me

    I am an Experimental Condensed Matter Physicist and Quantum Materials Scientist currently working as a Postdoctoral Researcher at Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL).

    My expertise spans quantum material growth, synchrotron and neutron scattering, and local-structure analysis. I use computational tools as part of the experimental workflow: to analyze large-scale scattering data, reconstruct atomistic models, and uncover hidden structure-property relationships in quantum materials.

    Industry Direction

    I am targeting roles where a deep experimental materials background becomes an industrial advantage: materials scientist, experimental physicist, neutron scattering scientist, and quantum materials researcher. I am especially interested in problems that connect synthesis, characterization, atomistic interpretation, and structure-property relationships.

    My day-to-day work has trained me to move across the full materials research loop: grow or prepare samples, design scattering experiments, analyze complex signals, validate physical models, and communicate the result clearly.

    Research & Technology

    My academic work focuses on Quantum Materials—specifically, how local disorder drives emergent phenomena like topological phase transitions. To solve these problems, standard software wasn't enough, so I built my own.

    I specialize in Reverse Monte Carlo (RMC) modeling and have developed open-source Python/C++ frameworks to reconstruct phonon dynamics from static experimental data.

    Current Focus: Quantum Materials & Scattering

    I am focused on how local structure, correlated disorder, and magnetic interactions shape functional behavior in quantum materials. My core experimental toolkit includes crystal growth, synchrotron scattering, neutron scattering, total scattering, and PDF analysis.

    Alongside the experimental work, I develop reusable Python/C++ tools for RMC modeling, phonon dynamics, magnetic pair distribution function analysis, and scattering-informed atomistic model validation.
